questions.question: Explain the purpose of build.gradle file at the project level in an Android application.
questions.hint: Focus on project-wide configuration rather than app-specific settings
keyPoints: Top-level build configuration file for the entire project
Configures Gradle plugin version for Android
Applies plugins to all modules in the project

questions.question: Describe the purpose and contents of the 'res' directory in an Android project.
questions.hint: Think about non-code resources needed for UI
keyPoints: Contains all resource files for the application
Holds menu resources and animation files

questions.question: What is the purpose of AndroidManifest.xml in an Android application?
questions.hint: This is the configuration file for the entire application
keyPoints: Declares the application components (Activities, Services, BroadcastReceivers, ContentProviders)
Specifies required permissions for the app
Declares minimum Android API level required

questions.question: Explain the Gradle Wrapper and its importance in an Android project.
questions.hint: Think about build consistency across different team members
keyPoints: Ensures consistent Gradle version across development machines
Eliminates need for manual Gradle installation

questions.question: Describe the structure of the 'java' directory in an Android project.
questions.hint: Remember this directory follows Java package conventions
keyPoints: Contains main source set in main/ subdirectory
Includes test directories: androidTest (instrumented) and test (unit)
Holds Activity, Service, and other component classes

questions.question: Explain the complete directory structure of an Android project created in Android Studio, detailing the purpose of each major directory and file.
questions.hint: Organize your answer from project root to app module details
keyPoints: app/ - Application module containing source code and resources

questions.question: Compare the project-level build.gradle and app-level build.gradle files, highlighting their differences and interdependencies.
questions.hint: Think about what is global vs what is module-specific
keyPoints: Project-level: defines global repositories and plugin versions
App-level: defines module-specific dependencies and configurations
App-level: declares dependencies for the specific module
Project-level: configures allprojects block for shared config
Interdependency: App-level uses plugins declared in project-level

questions.question: Discuss the role of AndroidManifest.xml in detail, explaining its key components and how they influence application behavior.
questions.hint: Think beyond just listing - explain how each component affects behavior
keyPoints: Declares all application components (Activities with intent-filters)
Identifies package name for the app
Can declare process names for components
Supports application themes and label definitions

questions.question: Explain in detail the organization of resources in Android, giving examples of different resource types and their purposes.
questions.hint: Give specific examples of files in each resource type
keyPoints: res/layout/ - XML layouts for UI design
res/drawable/ - Images, shapes, selectors for graphics
res/xml/ - Preference XML files and other configurations
Resource qualifiers for different screen densities (drawable-hdpi, drawable-xhdpi)

questions.question: In an Android project, if the package name is 'com.example.myapp', draw the complete directory structure showing where the MainActivity.java file would be located, along with its test directories.
questions.hint: Remember the package structure mirrors directory structure
keyPoints: Show MainActivity.java under the package directory
Show test/ directory at same level for unit tests
Show androidTest/ directory for instrumented tests
Indicate both test directories follow same package structure

questions.question: What is the purpose of settings.gradle in an Android project?
questions.hint: Think about what modules exist in the project
keyPoints: Specifies which modules to include in the build
Lists all sub-projects in the build

questions.question: Given an Android project structure, identify and explain the contents of the gradle directory and its wrapper files.
questions.hint: Look inside the gradle directory to understand wrapper mechanism
keyPoints: gradle-wrapper.jar - Executable JAR for Gradle invocation
Explains wrapper scripts (gradlew for Linux/Mac, gradlew.bat for Windows)

questions.question: Describe the complete app module directory structure in an Android project, including all subdirectories and their purposes.
questions.hint: Focus on the app module specifically, not the entire project
